evals.prompt: Create a new Earth2Studio DataSource called `RandomGaussian` that generates random Gaussian data on a 1-degree global lat/lon grid. It should accept time and variable parameters and return an xr.DataArray with dimensions [time, variable, lat, lon]. Don't connect to any remote store — just generate random data. Implement it end-to-end following Earth2Studio conventions (license header, type hints, prep_data_inputs, etc.). Put it in `earth2studio/data/random_gaussian.py`.
evals.expected_output: Creates a DataSource class that generates random Gaussian data on a global 1-degree grid. Follows Earth2Studio conventions including license header, type hints, proper imports, and uses prep_data_inputs for input normalization.
assertions: File has SPDX Apache-2.0 license header
Class implements DataSource protocol with __call__(time, variable) -> xr.DataArray
Uses prep_data_inputs to normalize time and variable inputs
Returns xr.DataArray with dims [time, variable, lat, lon]
Lat array is 181 values from -90 to 90, lon array is 360 values from 0 to 360
Has proper type hints on all parameters and return values
Generated code closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_1_target.py

evals.prompt: Create a new Earth2Studio ForecastSource called `RandomForecast` that generates random uniform [0, 1] forecast data on a 2.5-degree global grid (73x144). It should accept time, lead_time, and variable parameters and return an xr.DataArray with dimensions [time, lead_time, variable, lat, lon]. No remote connection — just random data. Put it in `earth2studio/data/random_forecast.py` following all Earth2Studio conventions.
evals.expected_output: Creates a ForecastSource class that generates random uniform forecast data on a global 2.5-degree grid. Follows Earth2Studio conventions including license header, type hints, uses prep_forecast_inputs.
assertions: File has SPDX Apache-2.0 license header
Class implements ForecastSource protocol with __call__(time, lead_time, variable) -> xr.DataArray
Uses prep_forecast_inputs to normalize time, lead_time, and variable inputs
Returns xr.DataArray with dims [time, lead_time, variable, lat, lon]
Lat array is 73 values from -90 to 90, lon array is 144 values from 0 to 360
Uses numpy uniform [0, 1] distribution instead of gaussian
Has proper type hints on all parameters and return values
Generated code closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_2_target.py

evals.prompt: Create a new Earth2Studio DataFrameSource called `RandomStations` that generates random weather station observations. It should produce 50 random stations per time/variable combination with columns: time, lat, lon, elevation, observation, variable. Use a pyarrow SCHEMA class attribute. Implement `__call__(time, variable, fields)` returning a pd.DataFrame. No remote connection — just random data. Put it in `earth2studio/data/random_stations.py` following all Earth2Studio conventions.
evals.expected_output: Creates a DataFrameSource class with SCHEMA attribute and __call__ returning pd.DataFrame. Generates 50 random station observations per time/variable with proper schema validation.
assertions: File has SPDX Apache-2.0 license header
Class has a SCHEMA class attribute defined as a pyarrow schema
SCHEMA includes fields: time, lat, lon, elevation, observation, variable
Class implements __call__(time, variable, fields) -> pd.DataFrame
Uses prep_data_inputs to normalize time and variable inputs
Generates 50 observations per time/variable combination
Has proper type hints on all parameters and return values
Generated code closely matches the structure in evals/targets/eval_3_target.py
